description_long: This object is a vintage stereo sound system, likely an early phonograph or gramophone, featuring a large, conical metal horn attached to a rectangular wooden base. The base is constructed from dark, polished wood with visible grain and has metallic hardware, including hinges, knobs, and levers. On the top surface, there is a raised section with a circular black turntable or platter, surrounded by several mechanical controls and switches. The horn, which projects from one side, is metallic and flared, designed to amplify sound acoustically. The overall design is boxy and robust, with a flat base and reinforced corners, suggesting durability and a classic, historical aesthetic. The object lacks any visible modern electronic components, emphasizing its mechanical and analog nature.
description: A vintage wooden stereo sound system with a large metal horn, mechanical controls, and a classic boxy design. The object features a dark wood base, metallic hardware, and a prominent sound-amplifying horn.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the metal horn and the rectangular wooden base with visible hardware.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the open mouth of the horn and the top-mounted controls and platter.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the wooden box, hinges, and the horn partially visible on the left.
description_view_3: Opposite end view focusing on the circular opening of the horn and the arrangement of top controls.
attribute_control_panel_layout: A set of mechanical controls including knobs and levers arranged on the top surface near the platter, with no visible labeling.
